short_description_of_the_course.label: Short Description of the Course
short_description_of_the_course.value: This course covers the design and implementation of programming languages, introducing techniques for formally specifying the design of programming languages (BNF grammars, typing rules, and operational semantics), metatheory (type soundness), and techniques for implementing typecheckers, interpreters, and code generators for a representative set of core programming languages.

requirements_of_entry.label: Requirements of Entry
value: COMPSCI2001 Introduction to Object-Oriented Programming (BSc / MSci Computing Science / Software Engineering students)
An introductory programming course that teaches Java (visiting students)
COMPSCI2003 Algorithmic Foundations 2 (BSc / MSci Computing Science / Software Engineering students)
An introductory discrete mathematics course that teaches predicate logic and inductively-defined structures (visiting students / joint honours students).
This course is for Honours students only.

timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.label: Timetable and length and frequency of teaching sessions
timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.value: In-person delivery. Two one-hour lectures and a one-hour lab each week.

course_aims.label: Course Aims
course_aims.value:
This course aims to introduce students to the concepts behind the design of programming languages in a variety of different paradigms (e.g., functional, object-oriented, and imperative), and to equip students with the tools needed to design and implement a programming language. Students will learn how to read and write formal descriptions of programming languages, including rigorous treatments of functions, variable binding, scoping, and variable substitution. Based on the formal descriptions, students will learn how to implement a typechecker, interpreter, and code generator for various representative core programming languages, and understand how the property of type soundness ensures that a program will not produce errors when it is run. 

After completing the course, students should be prepared to undertake project work in programming language design and implementation.

intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.label: Intended Learning Outcomes of Course
intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.value: By the end of the course students will be able to:

formative_assessment_feedback.label: Formative Assessment & Feedback
formative_assessment_feedback.value: Weekly lab exercises; verbal feedback given by lecturer and lab demonstrators.
